error.regex: (invalid PID number|nginx\.pid.*invalid|PID file.*not found|stale PID file|open\(\).*nginx\.pid.*failed|no such process.*nginx)

verdict.summary: PID file is empty, stale, or missing. Usually after unclean shutdown, manual file deletion, or containerized nginx.

dead_ends.action: Manually write a PID number into the PID file
dead_ends.why_fails: Writing an arbitrary PID causes nginx -s reload/stop to signal the wrong process. Let nginx manage its own PID file.

dead_ends.action: Delete the PID file and hope nginx recreates it
dead_ends.why_fails: nginx only writes the PID file on startup. Deleting it while running makes nginx -s commands fail until next restart.

workarounds.action: Kill any existing nginx processes and start fresh
workarounds.how: sudo pkill nginx; sleep 1; sudo nginx  # or: sudo nginx -s stop 2>/dev/null; sudo nginx

workarounds.action: Use systemctl which manages PID tracking automatically
workarounds.how: sudo systemctl restart nginx  # systemd tracks the PID independently of nginx.pid

workarounds.action: Verify the PID file path matches nginx.conf
workarounds.how: grep pid /etc/nginx/nginx.conf  # default: /run/nginx.pid; must match systemd ExecStart expectations
